---
id: water-cherenkov-particle-id
order: 40
title: Water Cherenkov Particle Identification
short_title: Water Cherenkov PID
status: planned
summary: Classify particle identity from Water Cherenkov event displays using ring topology, timing, and light-pattern features.
category: particle-identification
technical_area: classification
physics_area: neutrino event reconstruction
detector_type: Water Cherenkov
technical_method: Computer vision
track: Detector Reconstruction
label: Open Dataset Challenge
datasets:
  - water-cherenkov-pid-v0
metrics:
  - macro-f1
metric:
  label: Macro F1
  goal: Maximize
  documentation: /documentation/metrics.html#classification-metrics
facts:
  - label: Category
    value: Particle identification
  - label: Detector type
    value: Water Cherenkov
  - label: Technical method
    value: Computer vision
  - label: Timeframe
    value: Dataset definition pending
  - label: Dataset type
    value: Water Cherenkov event-display records
  - label: Challenge type
    value: Open dataset benchmark
  - label: Technical problem
    value: Ring-pattern classification
links:
  docs: null
  baseline: https://github.com/youngsam/doraemon_site/tree/main/baselines/water-cherenkov-particle-id
  leaderboard: null
bibliography: ../../bibliography.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: mathjax
---

## Scope

This planned challenge will evaluate particle identification from Water Cherenkov detector images and timing summaries. The benchmark follows the basic detector setting of large optical Cherenkov experiments such as Super-Kamiokande [@fukuda2003superkamiokande], but the public record will use a simplified open dataset schema.

## Protocol needs

The final protocol should define particle labels, calibration assumptions, train and hold-out splits, and class-balance handling. The primary metric is macro F1,

$$
F_{1,\mathrm{macro}} =
\frac{1}{K}\sum_{k=1}^{K}
\frac{2p_k r_k}{p_k + r_k},
$$

where $p_k$ and $r_k$ are precision and recall for class $k$.

## Leaderboard

The leaderboard is not open until the dataset record and baseline classifier are ready. The protocol should make detector-response assumptions visible, especially any timing smearing, dark-noise simulation, or event-display preprocessing.
